pages.full_text:
Robert had a lovely installation service with trumpets. Ted in the school, then a long, cold interesting walk back along the docks to our campsite at the __Dockside__ **
A pleasant campsite - helpful, information leaflets. Trees in the middle, a fence around with a boatyard on the other side
~~Sunday April 19th~~
Sunday April 19th
    We had a very cold night & then drove to the Deanery to camp at 20 Charlotte St. on the Grimley's drive. Their other visitors were cousins from Derbyshire with a Grimley pork pie, & John Hatton. The morning service in the Cathedral was beautiful. Robert preached. Then we had lunch at the Deanery. John & Charles were able to go up to the park & play & buy ice creams alone. We also climbed Cabot's Tower, with its steep winding staircase. The Deanery is a very pretty house & garden. Pillars in the hall.
    After lunch we set off home.
pages.notes: The strikethrough 'Sunday April 19th' appears to be a false start immediately corrected on the next line. 'Sunday April 19th' falls on a Sunday in 1992 and 1998 within the volume's date range (1989-2003); year cannot be confirmed from this page alone. The asterisks '**' after 'Dockside' appear to be a reference mark (possibly footnote or emphasis). 'Robert preached' and the 'installation service' suggest Robert is a clergyman being installed at Bristol Cathedral. 'Charlotte St' may be '20 Charlotte St' as written. 'Grimley pork pie' likely refers to a food item brought by the Grimley family.

pages.notes: Page is dominated by a glued-in Bristol multi-view postcard. Handwritten captions appear below and beside the postcard listing the scenes depicted. The annotation '^ The Grimley's Deanery' appears in the top-right margin, written vertically, likely referencing one of the postcard images. The first caption word is unclear — possibly 'Handpost' or 'Landscape' followed by 'from' but difficult to read clearly. There is no clearly visible handwritten page number.
